title: Dropbox app credentials should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don't need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

checks.message: Dropbox Access Token detected (sl. prefix). Dropbox access tokens grant full access to the linked Dropbox account's files and folders. Exposing this token allows attackers to read, download, modify, and delete all files in the Dropbox account, access shared files and folders, and impersonate the account owner. Revoke immediately at dropbox.com/account/connected_apps.

Dropbox App Key or App Secret detected in variable assignment. Dropbox app credentials (App Key and App Secret) identify and authenticate your Dropbox application. Exposing these credentials allows attackers to impersonate your application, generate unauthorized OAuth tokens for any user who has authorized your app, and gain access to their Dropbox files. Regenerate credentials immediately at dropbox.com/developers/apps.

Dropbox long-form credential detected. Long base64-like strings associated with Dropbox may represent access tokens, refresh tokens, or OAuth credentials. Exposing these allows unauthorized access to Dropbox files and account data. Revoke credentials immediately from dropbox.com/account/connected_apps.
